abort.no_compatible_transmitters: No radio frequency transmitter supports {frequency} {modulation} transmissions. Please add a compatible transmitter first.
abort.no_transmitters: No radio frequency transmitters are available. Please set up a device first.

data_description.transmitter: The radio frequency transmitter to use for sending commands.

user.description: Choose the radio frequency transmitter that will control the fan. Home Assistant assigns the fan a new RF identity that you will pair in the next step.

pair.title: Pair the fan
pair.description:
Put the fan into pairing mode now:

1. Set the fan's power switch to **0** (off).
2. Turn the speed dial to the **remote-control symbol**.
3. Set the power switch to **I** — a 5-second pairing window starts.
4. **Immediately** press Submit. Home Assistant sends a pairing burst for a few seconds; the fan's LED should blink red while it learns the code.

pair_failed.title: Pairing command failed
pair_failed.description: Home Assistant could not send the pairing burst. Check that the radio frequency transmitter is online, then press Retry.

test.title: Confirm the fan responds
test.description: Home Assistant sent a speed command. Did the fan start spinning? Press Finish to save, or Retry to pair again.

data_description.transmitter: The radio frequency transmitter to use for sending commands.

reconfigure.description: Choose the radio frequency transmitter for this fan. The fan keeps its existing RF identity, so no re-pairing is needed.
